plan.title: Model Context Protocol: The Local Backdoor
plan.styleGuide: Dark palette with neon accents, continuous rightward pan, cinematic vertical 9:16.
plan.music: Ambient electronic, low BPM, building energy.

segments.title: Segment 1
segments.visual: A moody, dark developer studio setup with a multi-monitor desk glowing softly with violet backlighting. In the foreground, an electric-violet network diagram shows an external AI agent connecting to a local host server. A red animated dotted arrow labeled 'Malicious Payload' shoots from the web, bypasses an abstract firewall graphic, and penetrates into a central glowing directory folder labeled 'Local Directory (~/)'. A circular radar-like scanning graphic sweeps continuously over the diagram, highlighting a blinking 'BACKDOOR DETECTED' warning sign.
segments.voiceover: Your local AI agents might be opening a backdoor to your computer.
segments.transitionInCue: None (Video starts directly with continuous rightward camera pan)
segments.transitionOutCue: Rightward camera pan with neon violet glitch transition

segments.title: Segment 2
segments.visual: A dark cyberpunk-inspired terminal with scrolling violet system logs in the background. In the foreground, a detailed split-screen side-by-side infographic comparison materializes. The left panel shows an animated timeline representing the 'MCP Stateless Spec' with independent JSON-RPC packets scrolling up. The right panel displays the 'CVE-2026-27825 Path Traversal' vulnerability: a glowing file directory tree chart displays the tool 'confluence_download_attachment' with a red path traversal arrow climbing up from a restricted folder and writing directly into '/usr/bin'.
segments.voiceover: While the MCP stateless spec simplifies scaling, tool vulnerabilities like CVE-2026-27825 allow critical remote code execution.
segments.transitionInCue: Rightward camera pan with neon violet glitch transition
segments.transitionOutCue: Rightward camera pan with crimson scanning-glitch transition

segments.title: Segment 3
segments.visual: A dark, atmospheric server rack backdrop with pulsing neon circuit boards. Overlaid in the foreground is an animated network graph showing a DNS rebinding attack mechanism: a browser icon resolving a domain first to an attacker-controlled external IP, which instantly rotates on an animated dial to bind directly to '127.0.0.1'. A crimson payload arrow bypasses the Same-Origin Policy, striking a highlighted icon of a local Go-SDK MCP server. A sharp warning label flashes at the bottom: 'SECURITY RATING: UNTRUSTED ENTITY'.
segments.voiceover: Unprotected Go SDK localhost servers risk DNS rebinding attacks. Treat local MCP servers as untrusted third-party entities.
segments.transitionInCue: Rightward camera pan with crimson scanning-glitch transition
segments.transitionOutCue: Rightward camera pan with terminal-flicker glitch transition

segments.title: Segment 4
segments.visual: A futuristic cyber-security operations control center display with faint grid lines. In the foreground, a horizontal block diagram titled 'Zero-Trust AI Agent Shield' assembles. Icons for 'Cursor IDE' and 'Windsurf IDE' send data streams through a glowing neon-green firewall layer labeled 'Zero-Trust Authentication Shield'. On the right, a high-tech glowing checklist slides on-screen with items '1. Lock Localhost Bindings' and '2. Restrict Tool Paths' lighting up with green checkmarks as they register 100% complete.
segments.voiceover: Audit your Cursor and Windsurf MCP endpoints and enforce zero-trust controls.
segments.transitionInCue: Rightward camera pan with terminal-flicker glitch transition
segments.transitionOutCue: None (Video ends with fade to black)

sources.title: DNS Rebinding Protection Disabled by Default in Model Context Protocol Go SDK
